heading: Definitions
label: 3052 Definitions
notes: Codificationand enacted identical sections. was repealed by .Pub. L. 110–234Pub. L. 110–246Pub. L. 110–234section 4(a) of Pub. L. 110–246

Effective DateSection and repeal of by effective , the date of enactment of , see , set out as a note under , Agriculture.Pub. L. 110–234Pub. L. 110–246Pub. L. 110–234section 4 of Pub. L. 110–246section 8701 of Title 7May 22, 2008

children.heading: Adjacent site
children.label: 1 Adjacent site
children.text: The term “adjacent site” means a site that borders a boundary line of National Forest System land.

children.heading: Cultural items
children.label: 2 Cultural items
children.text: The term “cultural items” has the meaning given the term in , except that the term does not include human remains.section 3001 of this title

children.heading: Human remains
children.label: 3 Human remains
children.text: The term “human remains” means the physical remains of the body of a person of Indian ancestry.

children.heading: Indian
children.label: 4 Indian
children.text: The term “Indian” means an individual who is a member of an Indian tribe.

children.heading: Indian tribe
children.label: 5 Indian tribe
children.text: The term “Indian tribe” means any Indian or Alaska Native tribe, band, nation, pueblo, village, or other community the name of which is included on a list published by the Secretary of the Interior pursuant to .section 5131 of this title

children.heading: Lineal descendant
children.label: 6 Lineal descendant
children.text: The term “lineal descendant” means an individual that can trace, directly and without interruption, the ancestry of the individual through the traditional kinship system of an Indian tribe, or through the common law system of descent, to a known Indian, the human remains, funerary objects, or other sacred objects of whom are claimed by the individual.

children.heading: National Forest System
children.label: 7 National Forest System
children.text: The term “National Forest System” has the meaning given the term in .section 1609(a) of title 16

children.heading: Reburial site
children.label: 8 Reburial site
children.text: The term “reburial site” means a specific physical location at which cultural items or human remains are reburied.

children.heading: Traditional and cultural purpose
children.label: 9 Traditional and cultural purpose
children.text: The term “traditional and cultural purpose”, with respect to a definable use, area, or practice, means that the use, area, or practice is identified by an Indian tribe as traditional or cultural because of the long-established significance or ceremonial nature of the use, area, or practice to the Indian tribe.
